name: content_addressed_storage_v1
description: BLAKE3 content-addressed storage operations: hashing, deduplication, integrity verification, garbage collection, and storage operations for adapterOS artifact management

entries.notes: BLAKE3 content-addressed storage: hash computation, deduplication, integrity verification, garbage collection, and storage layout operations for adapter bundles, datasets, and model weights

hash_computation.description: BLAKE3 hashing of files and compound objects. Streaming hash for large files. Hash of concatenated component hashes for compound artifacts.

deduplication.description: Content-level dedup across tenants and artifacts. Same content maps to same hash, stored once with multiple references. Storage savings calculations.

integrity_verification.description: Load artifact, recompute BLAKE3 hash, compare to stored address. Detect corruption, bit-rot, tampering, and truncation.

garbage_collection.description: Reference counting lifecycle: increment on registration, decrement on deletion. GC scheduling, safe deletion when refcount reaches zero, concurrent upload safety.

storage_operations.description: Put, get, exists, list, delete operations. Path layout with 2-char prefix sharding. Performance characteristics and filesystem considerations.

provenance.review_notes: Technical training data for BLAKE3 content-addressed storage in adapterOS. Covers the full lifecycle from hash computation through deduplication, integrity checks, garbage collection, and storage layout. Based on actual CAS patterns in adapteros-policy and adapteros-registry.

evaluation_gates: Same content always produces identical BLAKE3 hash (deterministic)
Different content produces different hash with overwhelming probability (collision resistance)
Deduplication stores identical content exactly once regardless of tenant count
Integrity verification detects any single-byte corruption
GC only deletes content with zero references
Concurrent upload during GC is safe due to reference acquisition before content write
Streaming hash produces same result as single-shot hash for same content
